# mitos

> Snapshot-fork sandboxes for AI agents on Kubernetes. Boots Firecracker
> microVMs, forks them via copy-on-write snapshots, and exposes the lifecycle
> through declarative CRDs and a sandbox API. The primary reader of every runtime
> error is a language model: errors are LLM-legible by contract.

This file is for AI agents. When a call against the sandbox API (forkd :9091 or
the standalone sandbox-server) or an SDK fails, the failure is a structured
envelope you can act on without parsing prose. Read the `code` and the
`remediation`; do not parse the `message`.

## Error envelope

Every error is:

```json
{
  "error": {
    "code": "<stable machine code>",
    "message": "<one-line summary>",
    "cause": "<call-site detail: ids, paths, ops; never a secret>",
    "remediation": "<an action you can take>",
    "context": { "sandbox": "sb-7" }
  }
}
```

- `code` is stable. Branch on it.
- `remediation` is always present and non-empty. Follow it.
- `cause` and `context` never contain a secret, token, or credential.
- JSON Schema: docs/api/error-schema.json
- Full normative catalogue: docs/api/errors.md

## Error codes (the full set)

- `invalid_json` (HTTP 400): the request body is not valid JSON. Send a JSON
  body matching the endpoint contract.
- `body_too_large` (HTTP 413): the body exceeds the size limit. Reduce the
  payload (file content is hex-encoded and bounded).
- `unauthorized` (HTTP 401): the per-sandbox bearer token is missing or invalid.
  Send `Authorization: Bearer <token>` with the per-sandbox token.
- `not_found` (HTTP 404): no such sandbox. Confirm the sandbox id exists and is
  Ready before calling. If the claim never went Ready, read its conditions in
  docs/conditions.md.
- `too_many_streams` (HTTP 429): the sandbox is at its concurrent-stream limit.
  Close an existing exec/run_code/PTY stream for this sandbox before opening
  another. Existing streams are unaffected.
- `exec_failed` (HTTP 500): the command could not be executed. Inspect `cause`;
  retry a transport error, otherwise check forkd logs for the guest agent state.
- `file_failed` (HTTP 500): a file operation failed. Confirm the path exists and
  is writable; inspect `cause`.
- `internal` (HTTP 500): an unclassified internal error. Retry; if it persists,
  inspect the forkd or sandbox-server logs.

## Examples corpus (in-context learning)

Real envelopes and the correct agent reaction.

### A call returns 404 not_found

```json
{"error":{"code":"not_found","message":"no such sandbox","cause":"no sandbox registered for id sb-7","remediation":"Confirm the sandbox id exists and is Ready before calling.","context":{"sandbox":"sb-7"}}}
```

React: do not retry the same id. Re-create or re-claim the sandbox, wait for
Ready, then retry. If you created it via a SandboxClaim, check its
`status.conditions` (docs/conditions.md) for why it is not Ready
(`NoCapacity`, `NoHuskPod`, `ActivateFailed`).

### A streaming exec is refused with 429 too_many_streams

```json
{"error":{"code":"too_many_streams","message":"the sandbox is at its concurrent-stream limit","cause":"sandbox sb-7 is at its concurrent-stream limit","remediation":"Close an existing streaming exec, run_code, or PTY session for this sandbox before opening another, or raise the forkd --max-streams-per-sandbox ceiling. Existing streams are unaffected.","context":{"sandbox":"sb-7"}}}
```

React: close one of your open streams for `sb-7` and retry; do not open more.
Other sandboxes and your already-open streams are unaffected.

### A file write fails with 500 file_failed

```json
{"error":{"code":"file_failed","message":"the file operation failed in the sandbox","cause":"open /work/out.txt: read-only file system","remediation":"Confirm the path exists and is writable; inspect the cause for the underlying error.","context":{"sandbox":"sb-7","path":"/work/out.txt"}}}
```

React: the `cause` names the underlying error (read-only path). Write to a
writable path instead; do not retry the same write.

### A call without a token returns 401 unauthorized

```json
{"error":{"code":"unauthorized","message":"the bearer token is missing or invalid for this sandbox","remediation":"Send Authorization: Bearer <token> with the per-sandbox token from the <name>-sandbox-token Secret."}}
```

React: attach the per-sandbox bearer token and retry. The token is never echoed
in any error field.

## SDK error types

- Python: `from mitos.errors import AgentRunError` (`.code`, `.cause`,
  `.remediation`, `.status`, `.context`).
- TypeScript: `AgentRunError` (`.code`, `.errorCause`, `.remediation`).

Both parse the envelope above and redact any reflected bearer token from
`cause` before you ever see it.

## Optional

- [Error catalogue (normative)](docs/api/errors.md): the single source of truth.
- [Condition reason codes](docs/conditions.md): asynchronous control-plane
  reasons on CRD status, joint with the error catalogue.
- [API v2 spec section 2.3](docs/api/v2-spec.md): the normative intent.
